item.title: University of Gothenburg Doctoral Position in Peace and Development Research 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Sweden on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The University of Gothenburg, one of Scandinavia’s leading research-intensive universities, has announced an exceptional doctoral opportunity for aspiring researchers interested in peace, development, migration, human rights, governance, and security studies. The institution is inviting applications for a fully funded Doctoral Student Position in Peace and Development Research with a specialized focus on Host-State Responses to […]

The post University of Gothenburg Doctoral Position in Peace and Development Research 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Sweden on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Gothenburg, one of Scandinavia’s leading research-intensive universities, has announced an exceptional doctoral opportunity for aspiring researchers interested in peace, development, migration, human rights, governance, and security studies. The institution is inviting applications for a fully funded Doctoral Student Position in Peace and Development Research with a specialized focus on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression (TNR).

This prestigious PhD opportunity is hosted by the School of Global Studies at the University of Gothenburg and forms part of an international research project examining transnational repression and the responses of host states across the Nordic-Baltic region.

The successful candidate will join a vibrant international research environment and contribute to cutting-edge scholarship on one of the most important emerging issues in global politics, migration, democracy, and human rights.

Deadline: 14 August 2026

Interested candidates must submit their complete application no later than 14 August 2026.

About the University of Gothenburg

The University of Gothenburg is one of Sweden’s largest and most respected higher education institutions. With approximately 58,000 students and over 6,800 employees, the university provides a dynamic environment for learning, innovation, and research.

Known for addressing major societal challenges through interdisciplinary knowledge and international collaboration, the university attracts talented researchers and students from around the world.

Its commitment to academic excellence, diversity, inclusion, and global engagement makes it an attractive destination for doctoral candidates seeking world-class research training.

About Peace and Development Research at the University of Gothenburg

Peace and Development Research at the University of Gothenburg is internationally recognized as a leading interdisciplinary research environment.

The department includes approximately 50 researchers who employ diverse theoretical and methodological approaches to investigate critical global challenges.

Research themes include:

  • Peace and conflict
  • Security studies
  • Human rights
  • Gender and development
  • Migration and diaspora studies
  • Global governance
  • Regional governance
  • Political economy
  • Resistance and social movements
  • International relations
  • Democracy and authoritarianism

The doctoral candidate will become part of this thriving academic community and contribute to internationally relevant research.

Understanding the Research Focus: Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression

The advertised doctoral project focuses on Transnational Repression (TNR), a growing phenomenon attracting significant international attention.

Transnational repression refers to actions undertaken by authoritarian governments to intimidate, silence, punish, monitor, or undermine dissidents, activists, journalists, and political opponents living outside their home countries.

The doctoral research will be conducted within a larger international project examining:

  • How transnational repression occurs across borders
  • How democratic host states respond to these threats
  • Legal frameworks addressing transnational repression
  • Public policy responses
  • Institutional awareness and preparedness
  • Protection mechanisms for vulnerable diaspora communities

Possible Research Areas

Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative research proposals connected to host-state responses to transnational repression.

Potential areas of investigation include:

1. Awareness Among Government Institutions

Research may examine awareness levels among:

  • Law enforcement agencies
  • Immigration authorities
  • Border control institutions
  • Cybersecurity agencies
  • National security organizations

2. Government Outreach to Vulnerable Diasporas

Candidates may investigate:

  • State engagement with migrant communities
  • Protection initiatives
  • Trust-building measures
  • Community resilience strategies

3. Collaboration Between Government and Civil Society

Research could focus on:

  • Partnerships between public institutions and NGOs
  • Advocacy networks
  • Human rights organizations
  • Community support structures

4. Reporting Mechanisms for TNR Incidents

Potential topics include:

  • Incident reporting procedures
  • Victim support systems
  • Accessibility of reporting channels
  • Institutional responsiveness

5. Investigation and Prosecution of Perpetrators

Candidates may explore:

  • Criminal justice responses
  • Legal frameworks
  • Judicial processes
  • International cooperation mechanisms

6. Other Relevant Topics

The university encourages applicants to propose innovative research ideas related to transnational repression and democratic governance.

Comparative Nordic-Baltic Research Framework

The doctoral project will adopt a comparative approach focusing on two or three Nordic-Baltic countries.

Possible case countries include:

  • Sweden
  • Norway
  • Estonia

Applicants are encouraged to justify their case selection and explain how comparative analysis can contribute to advancing academic understanding of transnational repression.

Structure of the Doctoral Programme

The doctoral programme spans four years of full-time study and leads to the award of a Doctoral Degree (PhD).

Programme Components

Coursework

The first year includes structured doctoral-level coursework designed to strengthen theoretical, methodological, and analytical competencies.

Research Activities

Three years are primarily devoted to:

  • Data collection
  • Fieldwork
  • Comparative analysis
  • Academic writing
  • Conference participation
  • Publication development

Dissertation

The programme culminates in the production of an original doctoral dissertation that makes a significant scholarly contribution to Peace and Development Research.

Employment and Funding

Upon admission, the successful candidate will be employed by the University of Gothenburg as a doctoral student.

Benefits include:

  • Fully funded doctoral position
  • Monthly salary according to University of Gothenburg regulations
  • Access to university resources and facilities
  • Participation in international research networks
  • Academic supervision by leading scholars
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Possibility of teaching experience

The university applies local doctoral salary agreements and provides employment benefits associated with Swedish higher education institutions.

Teaching and Departmental Responsibilities

Doctoral students may undertake departmental duties corresponding to up to 20% of full-time employment.

These responsibilities may include:

  • Teaching undergraduate courses
  • Assisting in graduate-level instruction
  • Research support activities
  • Administrative responsibilities

Such duties extend the total study period accordingly.

Required Qualifications

Applicants must satisfy both general and specific doctoral admission requirements.

General Eligibility

Candidates must have:

  • A Master’s degree or equivalent second-cycle qualification, or
  • At least 240 higher education credits including 60 second-cycle credits, or
  • Equivalent qualifications obtained internationally

Specific Eligibility

Applicants should possess at least 60 credits in one of the following areas:

  • International Relations
  • Peace and Conflict Studies
  • Global Development Studies

Equivalent academic backgrounds from Sweden or abroad will also be considered.

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Excellent written English
  • Excellent spoken English
  • Proficiency in relevant languages connected to selected case-study countries

Strong communication skills are essential for conducting comparative international research.

Application Assessment Criteria

Applications will undergo a highly competitive evaluation process.

Reviewers will assess:

Research Proposal

Applicants must submit a research proposal of up to 2,000 words that includes:

  • Research problem
  • Purpose and objectives
  • Research questions
  • Theoretical framework
  • Methodology
  • Research design
  • Data sources
  • Timeline
  • Harvard-style references

Academic Publications and Thesis

The selection committee will evaluate:

  • Master’s thesis quality
  • Academic publications
  • Research reports
  • Other scholarly works

Curriculum Vitae

The CV should demonstrate:

  • Academic achievements
  • Research experience
  • Professional experience
  • Language competencies
  • Relevant technical skills

Academic Performance

Grades obtained during undergraduate and postgraduate studies will be considered.

Interviews

Shortlisted candidates will be invited to participate in interviews.

Selection Timeline

The recruitment process follows the schedule below:

  • Application Deadline: 14 August 2026
  • Shortlisting Decisions: September 2026
  • Final Decisions: October 2026
  • Latest Starting Date: January 2027

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Research Proposal (maximum 2,000 words)
  2. Master’s Thesis
  3. Relevant Academic Publications or Research Outputs
  4. Curriculum Vitae (Europass format recommended)
  5. Academic Diplomas
  6. Academic Transcripts
  7. Evidence of Additional Qualifications
  8. Contact Details of Two References

Please note that recommendation letters will not be considered.

Living and Researching in Sweden

The University of Gothenburg provides support services for international students and researchers relocating to Sweden.

Benefits of living in Sweden include:

  • High quality of life
  • Strong research infrastructure
  • Excellent work-life balance
  • International academic community
  • Family-friendly environment
  • Safe and inclusive society

The university actively promotes diversity, equality, and inclusion across all aspects of academic life.

Why You Should Apply

This doctoral opportunity offers a unique chance to contribute to one of the most urgent and rapidly evolving areas of global governance and human rights research.

By joining the University of Gothenburg, successful candidates will gain access to a leading international research environment, receive full financial support throughout their doctoral studies, and contribute to scholarship with real-world policy relevance.

For aspiring researchers interested in migration, authoritarianism, human rights, democracy, and international relations, this fully funded PhD position represents an outstanding opportunity to launch a successful academic career while producing impactful research on a globally significant issue.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

For more information about this opportunity, Click here

More global PhD opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post University of Gothenburg Doctoral Position in Peace and Development Research 2026: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Sweden on Host-State Responses to Transnational Repression appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNICEF-Linked Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat) Opportunity in India 2026: Apply for a High-Impact Social Development Consultancy Position in New Delhi
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Professionals seeking to advance their careers in project management, social development, and international development now have an exciting opportunity to work on a high-impact initiative associated with UNICEF. Inductus Limited has announced vacancies for the position of Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat), offering experienced professionals the chance to contribute to meaningful development programmes while working within […]

The post UNICEF-Linked Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat) Opportunity in India 2026: Apply for a High-Impact Social Development Consultancy Position in New Delhi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals seeking to advance their careers in project management, social development, and international development now have an exciting opportunity to work on a high-impact initiative associated with UNICEF. Inductus Limited has announced vacancies for the position of Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat), offering experienced professionals the chance to contribute to meaningful development programmes while working within a dynamic consultancy environment.

This opportunity is particularly attractive for candidates with backgrounds in social sciences, economics, development studies, and international development who possess strong project management capabilities and a passion for driving social impact. The consultancy position is based in New Delhi, India, and offers a highly competitive compensation package that will be determined according to the selected candidate’s qualifications, experience, and salary history.

Overview of the Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat) Position

The Project Management Consultant role is being offered by Inductus Limited under a contract associated with UNICEF. The consultancy falls within the Social Development category and seeks qualified professionals capable of supporting programme implementation, project coordination, stakeholder engagement, monitoring activities, and strategic development initiatives.

The position provides an opportunity to work on projects that contribute to social development outcomes while collaborating with government institutions, development partners, technical experts, and other stakeholders.

Position Summary

  • Position Title: Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat)
  • Organization: Inductus Limited
  • Associated Contract: UNICEF
  • Location: New Delhi, India
  • Employment Type: Contract Consultancy
  • Sector: Social Development
  • Experience Required: Minimum 4 Years
  • Application Deadline: 14 June 2026

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The development sector continues to require highly skilled professionals who can manage complex projects, coordinate stakeholders, and deliver measurable impact. This consultancy offers professionals the chance to contribute to meaningful development work while enhancing their expertise in project management and programme implementation.

Successful candidates may gain valuable experience working on initiatives that align with national development priorities and international development objectives.

Key advantages of this opportunity include:

Benefits of the Position

  • Opportunity to work on a UNICEF-associated project
  • Competitive compensation package
  • Exposure to large-scale development programmes
  • Professional growth in project management and social development
  • Experience working with multidisciplinary stakeholders
  • Opportunity to contribute to public and social impact initiatives
  • Engagement with development sector experts
  • Potential for strengthening professional networks
  • Opportunity to enhance consultancy and programme management experience

Educational Requirements

Candidates interested in applying for this consultancy position should possess an academic background relevant to development and social policy.

Accepted educational backgrounds include:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Social Sciences
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Economics
  • Bachelor’s Degree in International Development
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Development Studies
  • Related academic disciplines relevant to the assignment

Applicants with additional certifications in project management, programme management, monitoring and evaluation, public policy, or development practice may possess an added advantage depending on the assignment requirements outlined in the Terms of Reference.

Professional Experience Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate a minimum of four years of relevant professional experience.

Relevant experience may include:

  • Project management
  • Programme implementation
  • Development consulting
  • Social sector projects
  • Public policy initiatives
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Development programme coordination
  • International development assignments
  • Government or NGO programme management

Candidates should be able to demonstrate measurable achievements and contributions from previous assignments.

Skills and Competencies

The vacancy announcement indicates that detailed skills and competency requirements are available within the official Terms of Reference (TOR).

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the TOR document to understand:

  • Technical competencies
  • Deliverables
  • Reporting requirements
  • Assignment deadlines
  • Travel expectations
  • Daily Subsistence Allowance (DSA) provisions
  • Required expertise
  • Expected outputs
  • Project management responsibilities

A thorough understanding of the TOR will significantly improve application quality and alignment with project expectations.

Location and Working Environment

The position is based in New Delhi, India’s capital city and one of the country’s most important centres for policymaking, development programming, and international cooperation.

Working in New Delhi provides opportunities to engage with:

  • Government ministries and agencies
  • International organizations
  • Development partners
  • Civil society organizations
  • Research institutions
  • Technical experts
  • Policy professionals

The location offers a dynamic environment for professionals interested in advancing careers in development and social impact sectors.

Compensation and Remuneration

Inductus Limited has stated that the gross compensation budgeted for the position is highly attractive.

The final remuneration package offered to successful candidates will depend on:

  • Academic qualifications
  • Professional experience
  • Relevant expertise
  • Technical competencies
  • Previous salary history
  • Assignment suitability

This approach ensures that compensation remains competitive and reflects the value brought by the selected consultant.

Important Dates

Candidates should pay close attention to the recruitment timeline.

Key Dates

  • Job Posting Date: 1 June 2026
  • Application Deadline: 14 June 2026

Given the relatively short application window, interested professionals are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials immediately.

How to Apply

Applicants are required to register and log in through the recruitment platform before submitting their applications.

Before applying, candidates should:

  1. Download and review the Terms of Reference (TOR).
  2. Verify that they meet all educational and experience requirements.
  3. Prepare an updated CV highlighting relevant achievements.
  4. Gather supporting documents and certifications.
  5. Complete registration on the recruitment portal.
  6. Submit the application before the deadline.

Carefully aligning your experience with the TOR requirements can significantly improve your competitiveness.

About Inductus Limited

Inductus Limited is facilitating the recruitment process for this consultancy opportunity associated with UNICEF. The organization operates within development and consultancy sectors, supporting projects that contribute to social impact and programme implementation.

The vacancy reflects growing demand for experienced project management professionals capable of delivering development outcomes in increasingly complex programme environments.

Final Thoughts

The Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat) position presents an excellent opportunity for experienced development professionals seeking to work on impactful social development initiatives linked to UNICEF-supported programming.

With attractive compensation, meaningful project responsibilities, and opportunities for professional growth, this consultancy can serve as a valuable career advancement opportunity for qualified candidates in project management, social sciences, economics, and international development.

Interested applicants should carefully review the Terms of Reference and complete their applications before the closing date of 14 June 2026.

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post UNICEF-Linked Project Management Consultant (MOYAS-MyBharat) Opportunity in India 2026: Apply for a High-Impact Social Development Consultancy Position in New Delhi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026: Fully Sponsored Regional Capacity Building Programme for Entrepreneurs in East Africa
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are now open for the IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026, a regional initiative under the Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I. Organized by the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD), this important programme seeks to strengthen the capacity of women […]

The post IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026: Fully Sponsored Regional Capacity Building Programme for Entrepreneurs in East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026, a regional initiative under the Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I. Organized by the Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD), this important programme seeks to strengthen the capacity of women and youth-led micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) across the IGAD region through specialized training and mentorship on digital trade, e-commerce, digital financial services, and cross-border market opportunities.

The programme will run from July to December 2026 across multiple IGAD Member States and is specifically designed to empower entrepreneurs with practical digital trade skills that will help them access regional and global markets, leverage technology for business growth, and benefit from opportunities created through the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA).

As Africa continues to accelerate digital transformation and regional economic integration, women and youth entrepreneurs remain central to building inclusive economic growth. However, gender inequalities, limited access to technology, digital literacy gaps, and restricted financial inclusion continue to affect the participation of women and youth in digital trade ecosystems. Through this initiative, IGAD aims to close these gaps by equipping MSMEs with the tools, knowledge, and mentorship required to compete successfully in today’s digital economy.

The training programme will use the Gender Action Learning System (GALS) methodology, a participatory and inclusive approach designed to strengthen gender-sensitive economic empowerment and business development. The initiative combines practical in-person workshops with online mentorship and continuous engagement sessions.

About IGAD and the HDMI Project

The Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) is a regional economic community focused on promoting peace, security, economic integration, and sustainable development across the Horn of Africa and East Africa region.

The Horn of Africa Digital Market Integration (HDMI) Project – Phase I is implemented under IGAD’s Economic Cooperation and Regional Integration Division (ECRID). The project operates in:

  • Djibouti
  • Ethiopia
  • Somalia
  • South Sudan

The broader objective of the HDMI Project is to strengthen regional economic integration by creating a more connected and inclusive digital market across the IGAD region.

The project supports:

  • Digital trade systems
  • E-commerce development
  • Cybersecurity
  • Digital transactions
  • Data protection
  • Cross-border digital services
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • Digital entrepreneurship

The programme aligns with:

  • African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA)
  • IGAD Vision 2050
  • IGAD Strategy 2026–2030

According to IGAD, digital transformation has the potential to:

  • Create jobs for women and youth
  • Expand market access
  • Support entrepreneurship
  • Increase competitiveness
  • Promote innovation
  • Strengthen regional integration
  • Improve trade efficiency

Why This Training Programme Matters

Digital trade is becoming one of the fastest-growing sectors in Africa’s economy. Businesses that can effectively use:

  • E-commerce platforms
  • Digital payment systems
  • Online marketing
  • Mobile money
  • Digital financial tools

are increasingly gaining access to larger markets and greater economic opportunities.

However, many women and youth entrepreneurs still face challenges such as:

  • Limited digital literacy
  • Lack of access to digital platforms
  • Financial exclusion
  • Poor access to market information
  • Limited knowledge of cross-border trade opportunities
  • Weak digital marketing skills

The IGAD Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade Programme is designed to address these challenges directly by helping MSMEs build practical skills and confidence in digital business operations.

Programme Objectives

The programme aims to:

  1. Strengthen the digital trade capacity of women and youth MSMEs
  2. Promote gender-sensitive participation in digital economies
  3. Increase access to digital financial services
  4. Support participation in e-commerce
  5. Improve financial literacy and business management
  6. Expand access to regional and international markets
  7. Facilitate inclusion in AfCFTA trade opportunities
  8. Build sustainable entrepreneur networks across the region

Training Structure and Format

The programme will be delivered in a hybrid format consisting of both in-person and online sessions.

1. In-Person Training Workshops

Participants will attend a three-day in-country workshop delivered by trained Gender Champions using the GALS methodology.

The in-person sessions are designed to:

  • Encourage active participation
  • Facilitate practical learning
  • Offer real-time feedback
  • Build hands-on experience
  • Strengthen peer learning
  • Enable networking among entrepreneurs

Participants will receive practical guidance on applying digital tools to real business situations.

2. Online Mentorship and Follow-Up Sessions

After the physical training workshops, participants will continue engaging through online mentorship sessions hosted via IGAD social platforms.

The online component will:

  • Reinforce learning outcomes
  • Provide ongoing mentorship
  • Support continuous knowledge exchange
  • Facilitate networking
  • Encourage collaboration among participants
  • Share additional digital trade resources

This blended learning approach ensures long-term impact and sustained entrepreneurial growth.

Training Locations

The programme will take place in the following cities across the IGAD region:

  • Djibouti – Djibouti
  • Addis Ababa – Ethiopia
  • Nairobi – Kenya
  • Mogadishu – Somalia
  • Juba – South Sudan
  • Port Sudan – Sudan
  • Kampala – Uganda

These locations will host the in-person training workshops between July and December 2026.

What Participants Will Learn

The training programme covers several key components of digital entrepreneurship and online trade.

1. Digital Financial Services

Participants will learn:

  • Mobile money systems
  • Digital payment platforms
  • Financial inclusion tools
  • Online transaction management
  • Secure financial practices

2. E-Commerce and Online Business Opportunities

Training topics include:

  • Selling products online
  • E-commerce platforms
  • Online customer engagement
  • Digital business models
  • Marketplace participation

3. Digital Marketing Strategies

Participants will gain skills in:

  • Social media marketing
  • Online branding
  • Digital customer outreach
  • Content marketing
  • Audience engagement

4. Financial Literacy and Business Management

The programme will help entrepreneurs improve:

  • Financial planning
  • Budgeting
  • Business sustainability
  • Revenue management
  • Entrepreneurial decision-making

5. Safe Participation in Digital Spaces

Participants will learn:

  • Cybersecurity basics
  • Digital safety practices
  • Online privacy protection
  • Secure digital communication

6. Cross-Border Trade and AfCFTA Opportunities

The programme will provide guidance on:

  • Regional trade opportunities
  • Export readiness
  • Cross-border e-commerce
  • Market access within Africa
  • AfCFTA opportunities for MSMEs

Benefits of the Programme

Selected participants will receive numerous benefits, including:

Capacity Building

Participants will develop practical digital trade skills that can directly improve business performance.

Access to Mentorship

The programme offers continued mentorship and support after the physical training workshops.

Networking Opportunities

Participants will become part of a growing regional network of entrepreneurs, trainers, and digital trade advocates.

Exposure to Regional Markets

Entrepreneurs will gain insights into regional and international market opportunities.

Improved Financial Inclusion

Participants will learn how to leverage digital financial tools and services for business growth.

Business Expansion Opportunities

The programme supports MSMEs in scaling their businesses through digital solutions and e-commerce strategies.

Participation at No Cost

Participation in the programme is completely free for selected applicants.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a woman or youth entrepreneur
  • Actively run a micro, small, or medium enterprise (MSME)
  • Possess a valid business license
  • Demonstrate interest in digital trade
  • Show commitment to expanding their business using digital platforms
  • Commit to attending the full three-day in-person workshop
  • Participate in online mentorship sessions

IGAD notes that priority will be given to women entrepreneurs.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Women entrepreneurs
  • Youth-led startups
  • MSME owners
  • E-commerce business owners
  • Small business operators
  • Digital entrepreneurs
  • Community business leaders
  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Traders seeking regional expansion
  • Entrepreneurs interested in AfCFTA opportunities

Expected Outcomes

After completing the programme, participants are expected to:

  • Gain stronger digital business skills
  • Improve business competitiveness
  • Increase participation in online trade
  • Expand market reach
  • Improve financial management
  • Enhance digital literacy
  • Access new regional markets
  • Strengthen cross-border business participation

The programme also seeks to contribute to broader regional goals such as:

  • Economic integration
  • Youth employment
  • Women’s empowerment
  • Digital transformation
  • Inclusive economic development

Required Application Documents

Interested applicants must submit:

  1. Completed online application form
  2. Brief motivation statement explaining why they want to join the programme
  3. Copy of business license
  4. Short business description
  5. Explanation of how they intend to use the acquired skills

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the IGAD Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade Training and Mentorship Programme is:

June 13, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early due to the expected high number of applications.

How to Apply

Applicants should complete the online application form through the official IGAD application portal.

Official Application Link:
https://igad.int/hdmicallforapplicationincountrytraining/

Important Notes

  • Participation is free for selected applicants
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted
  • Training logistics will be shared with successful participants
  • Full participation is expected during the programme

Visit HERE to learn more about the IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs.

Why Entrepreneurs Should Not Miss This Opportunity

The digital economy is rapidly transforming the future of trade in Africa. Entrepreneurs who can successfully adopt digital solutions are increasingly better positioned to:

  • Expand their customer base
  • Improve operational efficiency
  • Access financial services
  • Participate in cross-border trade
  • Increase revenue opportunities

This IGAD programme offers a rare opportunity for women and youth MSMEs in East Africa and the Horn of Africa to receive free high-quality training, mentorship, and regional networking support that can significantly strengthen their businesses and future growth prospects.

For entrepreneurs seeking to thrive in Africa’s growing digital economy and benefit from regional integration opportunities under AfCFTA, this initiative provides timely and practical support for long-term success.

Discover more global Training and Mentorship opportunities on OFY.

The post IGAD In-Country Training and Mentorship for Women and Youth MSMEs on Gender-Sensitive Digital Trade 2026: Fully Sponsored Regional Capacity Building Programme for Entrepreneurs in East Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece: Leadership Beyond Boundaries Programme for Emerging Mediterranean Leaders
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026, an international leadership and geopolitical training programme taking place in Ancient Olympia, Greece, from July 13–17, 2026. Organized by the Hellenic Foundation for European and Foreign Policy (ELIAMEP) in collaboration with the Institute of Global Affairs at The American College of Greece (ACG), this […]

The post ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece: Leadership Beyond Boundaries Programme for Emerging Mediterranean Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026, an international leadership and geopolitical training programme taking place in Ancient Olympia, Greece, from July 13–17, 2026. Organized by the Hellenic Foundation for European and Foreign Policy (ELIAMEP) in collaboration with the Institute of Global Affairs at The American College of Greece (ACG), this prestigious cross-border educational initiative brings together graduate students, young professionals, policy experts, researchers, diplomats, journalists, and future leaders from across the Mediterranean region and beyond.

Under the theme “Leadership Beyond Boundaries: Empowering the Next Generation in an Age of Geopolitical Flux,” the 2026 Summer Academy aims to equip emerging leaders with advanced knowledge, strategic leadership skills, negotiation techniques, and deeper understanding of global and regional geopolitical developments affecting Europe, the Mediterranean, the Middle East, and neighboring regions.

Hosted at the International Olympic Academy in Olympia, Peloponnese, Greece, the programme provides participants with an immersive academic and leadership experience in one of the world’s most symbolic locations for peace, international cooperation, dialogue, and intercultural exchange.

As geopolitical tensions, economic uncertainty, energy security concerns, migration pressures, climate challenges, and shifting global alliances continue reshaping the international landscape, programmes like the ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy have become increasingly important for preparing the next generation of leaders capable of navigating complex global realities.

The Academy combines academic rigor, leadership development, networking opportunities, practical workshops, and policy-oriented discussions to empower participants with the tools necessary to contribute meaningfully to international cooperation, diplomacy, strategic policymaking, and regional stability.

About the Organizers

Hellenic Foundation for European and Foreign Policy (ELIAMEP)

ELIAMEP is one of Greece’s leading independent non-profit think tanks focused on:

  • European affairs
  • International relations
  • Mediterranean studies
  • Foreign policy
  • Security and defense
  • Migration
  • Climate and sustainability
  • Regional cooperation

The organization has established itself as a major policy research institution in Europe and the Mediterranean, contributing to dialogue and analysis on some of the most pressing global challenges.

Institute of Global Affairs at The American College of Greece (ACG)

The Institute of Global Affairs at ACG supports research, education, and policy engagement related to:

  • Global governance
  • International diplomacy
  • Leadership development
  • Regional cooperation
  • Public policy
  • Geopolitical transformation

Together, ELIAMEP and ACG have successfully organized multiple editions of the Summer Academy, attracting participants from Europe, North Africa, the Middle East, and other parts of the world.

About the 6th ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026

The 2026 edition of the Summer Academy will focus heavily on leadership development in an increasingly uncertain and rapidly evolving geopolitical environment.

Participants will engage with:

  • Academics
  • Policymakers
  • Diplomats
  • Journalists
  • Researchers
  • Business leaders
  • International affairs experts

The Academy emphasizes interdisciplinary learning and cross-cultural dialogue while helping participants develop practical leadership and policy skills.

The programme is designed to foster:

  • Strategic thinking
  • Leadership capacity
  • International cooperation
  • Regional understanding
  • Policy analysis
  • Negotiation competence
  • Public speaking confidence
  • Collaborative problem-solving

Location and Dates

Programme Dates

July 13–17, 2026

Venue

International Olympic Academy
Olympia, Peloponnese, Greece

The venue is located near the historic site of Ancient Olympia, globally recognized as a symbol of peace, unity, and international cooperation.

The location itself offers participants a unique atmosphere for dialogue, reflection, and intercultural exchange.

Why This Summer Academy Matters

The Mediterranean region continues to experience major geopolitical transformations influenced by:

  • Strategic competition between global powers
  • Regional instability
  • Migration challenges
  • Climate change
  • Energy transitions
  • Security concerns
  • Economic uncertainty
  • Technological disruptions

Emerging leaders and professionals working in policy, diplomacy, governance, media, and civil society increasingly require:

  • Cross-border collaboration skills
  • Strategic foresight
  • Negotiation expertise
  • International leadership capabilities

The ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy addresses these needs directly through an intensive leadership and policy training programme.

Key Themes of the 2026 Summer Academy

1. Global Geopolitical and Geoeconomic Shifts

Participants will explore:

  • Shifting global power dynamics
  • Strategic competition
  • Emerging economic alliances
  • Regional instability
  • New geopolitical opportunities
  • International security trends

The programme examines how these developments affect:

  • Europe
  • The Mediterranean
  • The Middle East
  • Africa
  • International governance systems

2. The Role of Global and Regional Powers in the Mediterranean

The Academy will analyze how major actors such as:

  • European Union
  • United States
  • Russia
  • China
  • Gulf States
  • MENA countries

shape political, economic, and security developments across the Mediterranean region.

Participants will gain deeper understanding of:

  • Strategic interests
  • Regional influence
  • Diplomacy
  • International cooperation
  • Competition among powers

3. Europe’s Strategic Interests in the Region

Discussions will focus on:

  • European security
  • Migration management
  • Energy security
  • Trade partnerships
  • Regional resilience
  • EU foreign policy
  • Mediterranean governance frameworks

Participants will evaluate how Europe seeks to balance:

  • Stability
  • Cooperation
  • Economic interests
  • Human security
  • Climate priorities

4. Conflict Management and Policy Bridge-Building

One of the programme’s major components focuses on:

  • Diplomacy
  • Peacebuilding
  • Mediation
  • Inclusive governance
  • Regional integration
  • Sustainable conflict resolution

Participants will study:

  • Drivers of regional conflict
  • Policy negotiation frameworks
  • Mechanisms for dialogue
  • Multilateral cooperation strategies

Interactive Workshops and Practical Skills Training

The Academy includes highly practical leadership-focused workshops.

Strategic Foresight Skills

Participants will learn:

  • Scenario planning
  • Long-term strategic analysis
  • Anticipating geopolitical risks
  • Decision-making under uncertainty

Advanced Negotiation Techniques

Workshops will strengthen participants’ abilities in:

  • Diplomatic negotiations
  • Conflict mediation
  • Consensus building
  • Stakeholder engagement

Public Speaking and Media Training

Participants will receive training on:

  • Communication strategies
  • Public engagement
  • Media interaction
  • Policy communication
  • Leadership messaging

These practical sessions aim to strengthen participants’ confidence and leadership presence in professional and international environments.

Programme Structure

The Summer Academy combines:

  • Academic lectures
  • Interactive seminars
  • Policy discussions
  • Leadership workshops
  • Group activities
  • Networking opportunities
  • Collaborative learning sessions

Participants will engage directly with experts and peers from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is ideal for:

  • Graduate students
  • Young professionals
  • Emerging leaders
  • Diplomats
  • Researchers
  • Journalists
  • Civil society representatives
  • Policy practitioners
  • Think tank analysts

The Summer Academy particularly targets individuals interested in:

  • International relations
  • Diplomacy
  • Public policy
  • Mediterranean studies
  • European affairs
  • Global governance
  • Security studies
  • Geopolitics

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Hold at least a postgraduate or Master’s degree in a relevant field
  • Demonstrate leadership potential
  • Be proficient in English
  • Be 35 years old or younger

All programme sessions will be conducted in English.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents in English:

1. Statement of Purpose

Maximum 200 words explaining:

  • Why the Summer Academy is relevant to their professional development
  • Their motivation for applying
  • Their leadership interests

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

3. Proof of English Proficiency

Applicants are encouraged to ensure their documents clearly demonstrate:

  • Leadership experience
  • Academic preparation
  • Professional aspirations
  • Interest in international affairs

Participation Fees and Deadlines

Early Bird Application

  • Cost: €350
  • Deadline: March 31, 2026

Regular Application

  • Cost: €450
  • Deadline: June 15, 2026

What the Participation Fee Covers

The organizers will cover:

  • Accommodation
  • Full-board meals
  • Local transportation
  • Conference-related activities
  • Guided tours
  • Archaeological site visits

Participants are responsible for:

  • International travel expenses
  • Visa costs (if applicable)
  • Personal expenses

Benefits of Participating

Participants will gain:

Advanced Leadership Skills

The programme focuses heavily on practical leadership development and strategic thinking.

International Networking Opportunities

Participants will connect with:

  • Future leaders
  • Policymakers
  • Diplomats
  • Academics
  • Researchers
  • Journalists

Exposure to High-Level Experts

Sessions will be facilitated by renowned professionals and scholars in international affairs.

Enhanced Career Development

The programme strengthens participants’ professional profiles in:

  • Diplomacy
  • Public policy
  • International relations
  • Research
  • Governance

Cross-Cultural Collaboration

Participants will engage with peers from multiple countries and backgrounds.

Mediterranean Geopolitical Expertise

The Academy provides deep regional insights highly relevant to current international affairs.

Why Ancient Olympia Is a Powerful Venue

Hosting the Academy in Ancient Olympia carries symbolic significance because the site historically represents:

  • Peace
  • Dialogue
  • International cooperation
  • Shared human values

The environment encourages participants to reflect on:

  • Global leadership
  • Ethical governance
  • Cooperation across cultures

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the Official ELIAMEP Summer Academy Platform.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early, particularly to benefit from the reduced early bird fee.

Application Deadline

Regular application deadline:
June 15, 2026

Contact Information

For additional inquiries:
SummerAcademy@eliamep.gr

Why Young Leaders Should Apply

In today’s increasingly fragmented geopolitical environment, leadership requires:

  • Strategic awareness
  • Cross-cultural communication
  • Policy understanding
  • Negotiation competence
  • Ethical decision-making

The ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy offers emerging leaders a rare opportunity to:

  • Build these essential skills
  • Expand international networks
  • Learn from experts
  • Engage in meaningful dialogue
  • Strengthen their leadership capacity

For young professionals and graduate students seeking to contribute to regional cooperation, diplomacy, governance, and international policymaking, this programme offers a transformative educational and professional experience in one of the world’s most historic and inspiring locations.

Visit HERE to learn more about the ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece.

Discover more global Conference, Training, and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post ELIAMEP-ACG Summer Academy 2026 in Greece: Leadership Beyond Boundaries Programme for Emerging Mediterranean Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNITAR–FAO Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course 2026: Free Online Training Opportunity for Policymakers, Researchers, and Agricultural Trade Professionals in Africa
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR), in partnership with the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), is offering a fully funded online training programme titled Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN). This highly specialized course is designed to strengthen the capacity of policymakers, government officials, researchers, and agricultural trade […]

The post UNITAR–FAO Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course 2026: Free Online Training Opportunity for Policymakers, Researchers, and Agricultural Trade Professionals in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR), in partnership with the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), is offering a fully funded online training programme titled Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN). This highly specialized course is designed to strengthen the capacity of policymakers, government officials, researchers, and agricultural trade professionals across Eastern and Southern Africa.

Scheduled to run from 13 July 2026 to 14 August 2026, this five-week e-learning programme provides participants with a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of the complex relationship between agricultural trade, food security, nutrition, and sustainable development.

The course is offered completely free of charge and is delivered online, making it accessible to professionals across multiple African countries without the need for travel. Successful participants will receive a certificate of completion from UNITAR and FAO.

Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early as places are limited and participation is subject to a competitive selection process.

Why This Course Matters

Food security remains one of the most pressing development challenges facing Africa. At the same time, trade is increasingly recognized as a powerful tool for promoting economic growth, agricultural transformation, and poverty reduction.

The relationship between trade and food security has become a major focus of global development discussions, particularly as countries work toward achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), including the goal of ending hunger by 2030.

Through the 2014 Malabo Declaration, African governments committed to increasing intra-African agricultural trade and leveraging regional and international markets to stimulate agricultural development.

However, expanding trade alone does not automatically improve food security. Policymakers must carefully design trade policies that enhance agricultural productivity while ensuring access to nutritious food and supporting vulnerable populations.

This course addresses these critical issues by helping participants understand how trade policies can support food security, nutrition, and sustainable development.

About the Trade, Food Security and Nutrition Course

The Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course aims to strengthen institutional and individual capacity across Eastern and Southern Africa.

Participants will learn how to:

  • Analyze the relationship between trade and food security
  • Understand the effects of agricultural trade on nutrition outcomes
  • Evaluate trade policy measures
  • Formulate evidence-based trade policies
  • Negotiate trade agreements effectively
  • Assess food security implications of trade liberalization
  • Understand international trade governance frameworks
  • Promote policy coherence between trade and nutrition objectives

The course combines theoretical foundations with practical regional experiences and case studies relevant to Africa’s agricultural and economic realities.

Learning Objectives

By the end of the programme, participants will be able to:

1. Assess Trade and Food Security Challenges

Participants will gain the ability to:

  • Identify opportunities created by trade liberalization
  • Evaluate risks associated with increased market openness
  • Analyze food security outcomes resulting from trade policies

2. Understand Trade Policy Measures

Learners will explore:

  • Different types of trade interventions
  • Agricultural support policies
  • Trade facilitation mechanisms
  • Policy design considerations
  • Implementation challenges

3. Examine Global Trade Governance

Participants will study:

  • The World Trade Organization (WTO) Agreement on Agriculture
  • Global trade governance systems
  • Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  • International food security frameworks

4. Evaluate Trade and Nutrition Linkages

The course will help participants understand:

  • How trade influences food prices
  • The impact of trade on dietary diversity
  • Nutrient availability and food imports
  • Policy coherence between nutrition and trade agendas

Course Structure and Curriculum

The programme is divided into four comprehensive units.

Unit 1: Introduction to Trade and Food Security

Lesson 1.1: Definitions and Linkages Between Trade and Food Security

Topics include:

  • Trade fundamentals
  • Food security concepts
  • Economic linkages
  • Agricultural market dynamics

Lesson 1.2: Impacts of Trade on Food Security

Participants will examine:

  • Positive and negative impacts of trade
  • Household food access
  • Agricultural productivity
  • Market integration

Unit 2: Trade Policy Supportive of Food Security

Lesson 2.1: Trade Policy Measures and Agricultural Development

Areas covered include:

  • Tariffs
  • Trade restrictions
  • Agricultural support measures
  • Market interventions

Lesson 2.2: Design and Implementation of Trade Policy Measures

Participants will learn:

  • Policy formulation approaches
  • Implementation frameworks
  • Monitoring and evaluation mechanisms

Unit 3: Governance of Trade and Food Security

Lesson 3.1: Introduction to the Multilateral Trading System

Topics include:

  • WTO fundamentals
  • Global trade agreements
  • International trade governance

Lesson 3.2: Policy Space for Food Security in the Multilateral Trading System

Participants will explore:

  • National policy flexibility
  • Trade obligations
  • Food security safeguards

Lesson 3.3: Towards Improved Governance for Trade and Food Security

Focus areas include:

  • Institutional coordination
  • Governance reforms
  • Regional cooperation

Unit 4: Trade and Nutrition – Policy Coherence for Healthy Diets

Lesson 4.1: Trade and Nutrition Linkages

Participants will study:

  • Food systems
  • Nutrition outcomes
  • International food trade

Lesson 4.2: Trade in Food and Nutrients

Topics include:

  • Food diversity
  • Nutrient availability
  • Food affordability
  • Market access

Lesson 4.3: Strengthening Policy Coherence for Trade and Nutrition

Participants will learn strategies for:

  • Integrating nutrition objectives into trade policies
  • Supporting healthier diets
  • Aligning national development priorities

Course Delivery Method

The programme is delivered entirely online through UNITAR’s e-learning platform.

Duration

  • Five weeks
  • 13 July 2026 – 14 August 2026

Language

  • English only

Learning Format

Participants will engage in:

  • Self-paced learning
  • Reading assignments
  • Interactive discussions
  • Case study exercises
  • Assessment quizzes
  • Expert-led webinars

The course is facilitated by international experts with extensive practical experience in agricultural trade, food security, and development policy.

Benefits of Participating

Participants will gain numerous professional and technical benefits, including:

Professional Development

  • Enhanced trade policy expertise
  • Improved understanding of food security issues
  • Practical knowledge of agricultural trade governance
  • Increased capacity for policy formulation

Networking Opportunities

  • Interaction with professionals across Africa
  • Access to international experts
  • Exposure to regional perspectives and experiences

Career Advancement

  • Strengthened policy analysis skills
  • Greater capacity to influence trade and food security decisions
  • Enhanced professional credentials

Certification

Participants who successfully complete all assignments and assessments will receive an official Certificate of Completion from UNITAR and FAO.

Selection Process

Because the course is offered free of charge, spaces are limited.

Selection will be conducted by FAO based on:

  • Relevance of applicant’s professional role
  • Geographic representation
  • Gender balance considerations
  • Potential impact of acquired knowledge

Priority will be given to professionals who influence agricultural trade policies and food security programmes at regional, national, or sector levels.

Applicants selected for participation may also be required to obtain approval from their direct supervisors before receiving final confirmation.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 15 June 2026

Why You Should Apply

Trade and food security are increasingly interconnected issues shaping the future of agriculture, economic development, and nutrition across Africa.

This UNITAR–FAO programme offers a rare opportunity to learn directly from leading international experts while gaining practical skills that can immediately be applied within government institutions, research organizations, development agencies, and the private sector.

With no participation fee, expert-led instruction, flexible online learning, and an internationally recognized certificate, the Trade, Food Security and Nutrition Course is an outstanding professional development opportunity for anyone working at the intersection of agriculture, trade, food systems, and public policy.

Apply Here

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global training opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

The post UNITAR–FAO Trade, Food Security and Nutrition (TFSN) Course 2026: Free Online Training Opportunity for Policymakers, Researchers, and Agricultural Trade Professionals in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme 2026 in Nigeria: Paid Internship for Women in Sustainable Energy, Climate Finance and Renewable Energy
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The United Nations Office for Project Services (UNOPS), through Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL), is inviting applications for the 2026 STEM Trainee Programme in Nigeria. This six-month internship programme is designed to empower young women pursuing careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) by providing practical experience in sustainable energy, climate finance, policy development, […]

The post UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme 2026 in Nigeria: Paid Internship for Women in Sustainable Energy, Climate Finance and Renewable Energy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office for Project Services (UNOPS), through Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL), is inviting applications for the 2026 STEM Trainee Programme in Nigeria. This six-month internship programme is designed to empower young women pursuing careers in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) by providing practical experience in sustainable energy, climate finance, policy development, and renewable energy implementation.

The programme offers an exceptional opportunity for women in Nigeria to gain hands-on exposure to one of the world’s fastest-growing sectors while contributing to the global transition toward clean and sustainable energy.

Applications are open until 12 June 2026.

About the UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme

Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL), hosted by UNOPS, works globally to accelerate progress toward universal energy access, renewable energy adoption, climate action, and sustainable development.

The STEM Trainee Programme was created to increase women’s participation in the energy sector by providing practical training, professional development opportunities, and direct engagement with clean energy projects.

The initiative recognizes that women remain underrepresented in energy-related careers and decision-making positions despite being disproportionately affected by energy poverty and climate-related challenges.

Through this traineeship, participants will gain valuable technical and professional skills that can help launch successful careers in renewable energy, climate finance, sustainable development, and energy policy.

Why This Programme Matters

The global energy transition requires diverse talent and inclusive leadership.

Women continue to face barriers to entry and advancement within the energy sector. By investing in young women through targeted STEM opportunities, SEforALL seeks to:

  • Promote gender equality in the energy sector
  • Build a pipeline of female energy professionals
  • Strengthen youth participation in sustainable development
  • Support climate action and energy access initiatives
  • Increase women’s representation in energy decision-making processes
  • Develop future leaders in renewable energy and climate finance

What Participants Will Gain

The STEM Trainee Programme combines technical learning, project experience, professional development, and policy engagement.

Participants will benefit from:

Professional Development Training

Trainees will receive structured training in:

  • Career development
  • Resume and CV writing
  • Interview preparation
  • Networking skills
  • Leadership development
  • Professional mentorship
  • Communication skills
  • Social media engagement

Technical Energy Sector Training

Participants will gain practical knowledge in:

  • Sustainable energy systems
  • Renewable energy implementation
  • Energy access projects
  • Climate finance mechanisms
  • Gender-responsive energy programming
  • Energy project operations
  • Site assessment methodologies
  • Project monitoring and evaluation
  • Data management and reporting

Climate Finance and Financial Modelling

Trainees will learn:

  • Financial modelling techniques
  • Project bankability assessment
  • Capital structures
  • Grant financing
  • Debt financing
  • Equity financing
  • Sustainable energy investment mechanisms
  • Clean energy financing platforms

Real-World Project Experience

Participants will work alongside:

  • Government officials
  • Energy sector professionals
  • Development partners
  • Financial institutions
  • Renewable energy experts
  • Policy stakeholders

Programme Structure

The six-month traineeship consists of two major components.

1. Virtual Learning and Professional Development

This component focuses on:

  • Soft skills training
  • Technical capacity building
  • Climate finance education
  • Professional growth
  • Leadership development

2. Practical Technical Experience

Participants will be actively involved in:

  • Energy access projects
  • Technical implementation activities
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Site visits
  • Energy audits
  • Data collection exercises
  • Monitoring activities
  • Project coordination

Key Responsibilities of STEM Trainees

Technical Support and Operations

Trainees will:

  • Support implementation of energy projects
  • Assist in coordinating project activities
  • Participate in stakeholder meetings
  • Conduct energy-related field activities
  • Support site assessments and audits

Sustainable Energy Finance Platform Support

Participants will:

  • Validate project information
  • Support financing platform operations
  • Assist in training development
  • Support knowledge-sharing activities
  • Help monitor financing commitments

Monitoring and Data Analysis

Responsibilities include:

  • Conducting market research
  • Collecting project data
  • Supporting project monitoring
  • Analysing energy sector information
  • Preparing reports and insights

Climate Finance, Policy and Advocacy

Trainees will:

  • Support policy advocacy efforts
  • Engage with energy stakeholders
  • Learn about climate finance mechanisms
  • Promote sustainable energy financing
  • Participate in awareness campaigns

Communications and Social Media

Participants will:

  • Develop digital content
  • Promote project visibility
  • Support advocacy campaigns
  • Create social media materials
  • Produce awareness content

Skills Participants Will Develop

The programme emphasizes building competencies in:

  • Academic writing
  • Report writing
  • Data analysis
  • Energy policy
  • Problem solving
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Project management
  • Communication
  • Digital advocacy
  • Research methodologies

Previous Programme Success

SEforALL has successfully implemented similar traineeship programmes in:

  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Panama
  • Sierra Leone
  • Tanzania

These initiatives have helped young women build careers in sustainable energy while contributing to national and global development priorities.

About Sustainable Energy for All (SEforALL)

Founded in 2011 by former UN Secretary-General Ban Ki-moon, Sustainable Energy for All is a global organization dedicated to accelerating progress toward:

  • Universal energy access
  • Renewable energy deployment
  • Energy efficiency
  • Climate action
  • Sustainable development

The organization works with governments, development partners, private sector actors, and civil society organizations worldwide.

Why You Should Apply

This internship offers a rare opportunity to work at the intersection of:

  • Sustainable energy
  • Climate finance
  • Renewable energy
  • Public policy
  • International development
  • Gender equality
  • Youth empowerment

Participants will gain practical experience, build professional networks, and develop skills that are increasingly in demand across the global energy sector.

For women interested in contributing to climate solutions while building a meaningful career, this traineeship provides an excellent foundation.

Application Deadline

12 June 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline.

Apply Here 

For more information on this training visit here

Discover more training opportunities on OFY here 

The post UNOPS STEM Trainee Programme 2026 in Nigeria: Paid Internship for Women in Sustainable Energy, Climate Finance and Renewable Energy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher Position 2026 in London: Join a Leading African Governance and Policy Research Institution
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
For professionals passionate about African governance, public policy, development, democracy, and data-driven research, the Mo Ibrahim Foundation is offering an exciting opportunity to join its research team as a Researcher based in London. This position provides a unique chance to contribute to some of the most influential governance and development initiatives focused on Africa, including […]

The post Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher Position 2026 in London: Join a Leading African Governance and Policy Research Institution appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
For professionals passionate about African governance, public policy, development, democracy, and data-driven research, the Mo Ibrahim Foundation is offering an exciting opportunity to join its research team as a Researcher based in London.

This position provides a unique chance to contribute to some of the most influential governance and development initiatives focused on Africa, including the internationally recognized Ibrahim Index of African Governance (IIAG) and the Ibrahim Forum Report.

The role is ideal for talented researchers who are eager to combine rigorous data analysis, policy research, report writing, and stakeholder engagement while working within a globally respected organization committed to improving governance and leadership across Africa.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, making early submission highly advisable.

About the Mo Ibrahim Foundation

The Mo Ibrahim Foundation (MIF) is one of the world’s leading organizations focused on improving governance and leadership across Africa.

The Foundation is widely recognized for producing high-quality research, data, and analysis that inform policy discussions and support evidence-based decision-making throughout the continent.

Through its flagship initiatives, the Foundation promotes:

  • Good governance
  • Accountability
  • Leadership excellence
  • Sustainable development
  • Democratic participation
  • Economic transformation
  • Rule of law
  • Public sector effectiveness

Its work influences policymakers, governments, researchers, development organizations, academics, and civil society leaders across Africa and beyond.

About the Researcher Position

The Researcher will join the Foundation’s dedicated research team and support the organization’s core research and policy activities.

This role offers significant exposure to major governance and development issues affecting Africa today, while providing opportunities to work with large datasets, contribute to high-profile publications, and participate in international policy discussions.

The successful candidate will support two of the Foundation’s most important initiatives:

The Ibrahim Index of African Governance (IIAG)

The IIAG is one of the most comprehensive governance measurement tools for Africa.

The index evaluates governance performance across African countries by assessing numerous indicators related to:

  • Security
  • Rule of law
  • Participation
  • Rights
  • Economic opportunity
  • Human development
  • Public administration
  • Transparency and accountability

The Researcher will play a key role in supporting the ongoing development and analysis of this influential governance index.

The Ibrahim Forum Report

The Ibrahim Forum Report is a flagship publication that examines major issues shaping Africa’s future.

Topics frequently explored include:

  • Climate change
  • Economic development
  • Development finance
  • Governance reform
  • Security and conflict
  • Migration
  • Democracy
  • Youth development
  • Institutional strengthening

The Researcher will contribute to the production and dissemination of these important reports.

Key Responsibilities

The Researcher position offers a broad and intellectually stimulating portfolio of responsibilities.

1. Conducting Research and Data Analysis

A major component of the role involves conducting desk-based research and producing evidence-based analysis.

Responsibilities include:

  • Collecting data from multiple sources
  • Analyzing quantitative and qualitative information
  • Producing visualizations and analytical outputs
  • Synthesizing findings from diverse datasets

The successful candidate will support the Foundation’s commitment to rigorous and credible research.

2. Writing Literature Reviews and Research Papers

The Researcher will draft a variety of research products covering critical issues affecting Africa.

Potential research topics include:

  • Climate policy
  • Development finance
  • Security and stability
  • Democratic governance
  • Migration trends
  • Rule of law
  • Public sector performance
  • Economic development

The role requires the ability to transform complex information into clear, accessible, and policy-relevant insights.

3. Supporting Report Production

The successful candidate will assist with the preparation of:

  • Research reports
  • Policy briefs
  • Presentations
  • Background papers
  • Governance assessments

These outputs contribute directly to the Foundation’s influence within policy and development circles.

4. Contributing to the Ibrahim Index of African Governance

The Researcher will help:

  • Calculate governance indicators
  • Analyze governance trends
  • Improve methodologies
  • Develop thematic assessments
  • Identify emerging governance issues

This work helps maintain the IIAG’s reputation as a leading governance measurement framework.

5. Identifying New Data Sources

The Foundation continuously seeks innovative ways to strengthen its evidence base.

The Researcher will:

  • Monitor emerging datasets
  • Evaluate governance indicators
  • Identify new sources of information
  • Support data quality improvements

This work ensures that Foundation publications remain relevant and authoritative.

6. Monitoring Policy and Governance Developments

The role requires staying informed about developments affecting Africa.

Researchers will regularly monitor:

  • News and media coverage
  • Academic publications
  • Government reports
  • International development updates
  • Governance-related events

This helps inform ongoing research and advocacy efforts.

7. Supporting Advocacy and Dissemination

Research findings are most impactful when they reach decision-makers.

The Researcher will contribute to:

  • Dissemination strategies
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Knowledge sharing initiatives
  • Policy discussions
  • Public events

This provides exposure to both research and advocacy dimensions of the Foundation’s work.

8. Representing the Foundation

The successful candidate may represent the Foundation at:

  • Conferences
  • Research forums
  • Policy events
  • Governance discussions
  • Development meetings

This offers valuable opportunities to build professional networks and engage with leaders across sectors.

9. Supporting Major Foundation Events

The role includes logistical and research support for key Foundation initiatives, including the prestigious:

Ibrahim Governance Weekend

This biennial event brings together:

  • Heads of state
  • Policymakers
  • Development leaders
  • Academics
  • Civil society representatives
  • Business leaders

Participation in such events provides exceptional professional exposure.

Qualifications Required

Applicants must possess:

Educational Requirements

  • A postgraduate degree in a relevant field

Relevant disciplines may include:

  • Public Policy
  • Political Science
  • International Development
  • Economics
  • Governance Studies
  • International Relations
  • African Studies
  • Sociology
  • Public Administration

Professional Experience

Candidates should have:

  • 2–4 years of research or analytical experience
  • Internship experience may count toward this requirement

Experience should demonstrate:

  • Research capability
  • Analytical thinking
  • Policy understanding
  • Report writing skills

Essential Skills and Competencies

Successful applicants should possess:

Communication Skills

  • Excellent written English
  • Strong verbal communication abilities
  • Presentation skills
  • Research writing expertise

Technical Skills

  • Advanced Excel proficiency
  • Microsoft Office expertise
  • Experience handling large datasets
  • Data visualization capabilities

Analytical Skills

  • Strong numerical literacy
  • Ability to interpret complex data
  • Evidence-based decision-making capabilities

Research Software Skills

Knowledge of the following is considered a strong advantage:

  • Python
  • R
  • Tableau
  • Statistical analysis tools

These skills are increasingly valuable for governance and policy research.

Additional Language Skills

Fluency in the following languages is highly desirable:

  • French
  • Arabic
  • Portuguese

These languages support engagement across different regions of Africa.

Professional Attributes

The Foundation seeks candidates who are:

  • Detail-oriented
  • Organized
  • Adaptable
  • Collaborative
  • Curious and eager to learn
  • Diplomatic in multicultural environments
  • Able to work under deadlines

Location and Working Arrangements

The position is based in:

Marylebone, London, United Kingdom

Additional considerations include:

  • Full-time employment
  • Flexible working hours may be required
  • International travel may be required
  • Salary is competitive and based on experience

Application Deadline

Deadline: Rolling Applications

There is no fixed closing date.

Because applications are reviewed continuously, interested candidates should apply as early as possible.

How to Apply

Applicants should submit the following documents:

Required Documents

  1. A one-page cover letter explaining:
    • Interest in the role
    • Relevant qualifications
    • Professional experience
  2. An updated CV detailing:
    • Academic background
    • Employment history
    • Relevant skills and achievements
  3. Salary expectations
  4. Current notice period

Submission Method

Applications should be sent via email to:

research@moibrahimfoundation.org

Email Subject Line

Use: [Your Name] – Researcher Application

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Why You Should Apply

The Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher position offers a rare opportunity to contribute to some of the most influential governance and policy research initiatives focused on Africa.

Professionals in this role will gain:

  • Experience working on globally respected governance datasets
  • Exposure to high-level policy discussions
  • Opportunities to contribute to influential reports
  • Access to leading experts in governance and development
  • Experience engaging with policymakers and international stakeholders
  • Career growth within one of Africa’s most respected policy institutions

For researchers committed to advancing evidence-based governance and sustainable development across Africa, this role offers both meaningful impact and exceptional professional development.

For more information, read the official call for applications

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Mo Ibrahim Foundation Researcher Position 2026 in London: Join a Leading African Governance and Policy Research Institution appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: The Applied Data Fellowship 2026–2027: Launch Your Career in Data, AI, Public Policy, and Social Impact Through the University of Chicago Harris School of Public Policy
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
In today’s rapidly evolving world, data-driven decision-making is transforming governments, nonprofits, research institutions, and organizations working to solve society’s most pressing challenges. From artificial intelligence and machine learning to public policy innovation and operational transformation, there is growing demand for professionals who can bridge technical expertise with real-world impact. The Applied Data Fellowship (ADF), hosted […]

The post The Applied Data Fellowship 2026–2027: Launch Your Career in Data, AI, Public Policy, and Social Impact Through the University of Chicago Harris School of Public Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In today’s rapidly evolving world, data-driven decision-making is transforming governments, nonprofits, research institutions, and organizations working to solve society’s most pressing challenges. From artificial intelligence and machine learning to public policy innovation and operational transformation, there is growing demand for professionals who can bridge technical expertise with real-world impact.

The Applied Data Fellowship (ADF), hosted through the Harris School of Public Policy at the University of Chicago, offers a unique opportunity for aspiring data professionals, policy analysts, economists, and technical leaders to apply their skills where they matter most.

Designed for highly talented graduate students and emerging professionals, the Applied Data Fellowship places participants directly inside mission-driven organizations for a full year of hands-on work, mentorship, training, and professional development.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, allowing candidates to apply throughout the year and be considered for future fellowship cohorts.
