For those seeking to build a meaningful career at the intersection of data, technology, artificial intelligence, policy, and social impact, the Applied Data Fellowship offers an exceptional launchpad.

What Is the Applied Data Fellowship?

The Applied Data Fellowship is a one-year, full-time professional fellowship that connects highly skilled graduate-level talent with organizations tackling complex societal challenges.

Unlike traditional academic fellowships that focus primarily on research, ADF emphasizes practical implementation and real-world impact.

Fellows become embedded within partner organizations, where they contribute directly to projects involving:

  • Data analytics
  • Artificial intelligence
  • Machine learning
  • Public policy implementation
  • Operational improvement
  • Strategic decision-making
  • Research and evaluation
  • Data infrastructure development
  • Program management

The fellowship is designed for individuals who want to use data and technology to improve public systems, strengthen institutions, and create measurable social impact.

Who Are Applied Data Fellows?

Applied Data Fellows are more than data analysts.

They are problem-solvers, strategists, builders, innovators, and implementation specialists who combine technical expertise with a commitment to public service and mission-driven work.

Fellows often come from leading graduate programs in:

  • Public Policy
  • Data Science
  • Economics
  • Statistics
  • Computer Science
  • Social Sciences
  • Information Systems
  • Quantitative Research
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Related analytical disciplines

While every Fellow brings unique strengths, they share a common goal: using data and technology to solve meaningful problems.

Types of Roles Fellows Can Perform

One of the strengths of the Applied Data Fellowship is the diversity of roles available to participants.

1. Data Scientist or Data Analyst

Fellows may focus on extracting insights from large datasets and helping organizations make evidence-based decisions.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Data cleaning and preparation
  • Statistical analysis
  • Predictive modeling
  • Machine learning implementation
  • Dashboard development
  • Performance evaluation

Common tools include:

  • Python
  • R
  • SQL
  • Tableau
  • Power BI

2. Program Manager

Some Fellows work in operational and strategic roles.

These positions focus on:

  • Stakeholder management
  • Project coordination
  • Program implementation
  • Performance tracking
  • Strategic planning

Program managers help ensure that data insights translate into real-world outcomes.

3. Data Strategist (“Data Shaman”)

The fellowship recognizes that many impactful professionals operate between technical and operational domains.

These Fellows:

  • Bridge data and strategy
  • Improve organizational workflows
  • Support implementation efforts
  • Reduce operational bottlenecks
  • Facilitate adoption of technology solutions

This hybrid role combines analytical thinking with organizational leadership.

Technical Skills Valued by the Fellowship

The Applied Data Fellowship seeks applicants with expertise or interest in areas such as:

Programming and Analytics

  • Python
  • R
  • SQL

Data Visualization

  • Tableau
  • Power BI

Artificial Intelligence

  • Machine Learning
  • AI Adoption
  • AI/ML Operations

Data Engineering

  • Data Infrastructure
  • Data Management Systems

Geospatial Analytics

  • GIS Applications

Leadership and Operations

  • Program Management
  • Stakeholder Engagement
  • Strategic Planning

Applicants do not necessarily need mastery of every skill area, but strong technical or operational capabilities are highly valued.

A Fellowship Focused on Learning and Growth

A defining feature of the Applied Data Fellowship is its emphasis on continuous learning.

The fellowship is intentionally structured to ensure that Fellows continue developing throughout the year.

One-on-One Mentorship

Each Fellow receives dedicated coaching and mentorship.

Mentors help participants:

  • Navigate challenges
  • Build technical skills
  • Expand professional networks
  • Develop career strategies
  • Strengthen leadership capabilities

Weekly Upskilling Workshops

Fellows participate in hands-on training sessions designed to improve practical skills.

Topics may include:

  • Advanced analytics
  • Machine learning
  • Data visualization
  • Public sector technology
  • Policy analytics
  • Project implementation

These workshops provide immediate value to ongoing project work.

Fireside Chats with Leaders

Participants gain direct access to leaders from:

  • Government agencies
  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Research institutions
  • Technology companies
  • Public policy organizations

These conversations provide valuable insight into leadership, innovation, and career development.

Technology and AI Demonstrations

As AI continues to reshape industries, Fellows receive exposure to emerging technologies through regular demonstrations and discussions.

These sessions help Fellows understand:

  • AI applications
  • Emerging tools
  • Public-sector technology trends
  • Responsible AI implementation

Career Outcomes: Why the Applied Data Fellowship Stands Out

The Applied Data Fellowship has established a strong record of helping participants launch impactful careers.

Program statistics highlight its success:

70% Retention Rate

Approximately 70% of Fellows are retained full-time by their host organizations after completing the fellowship.

This demonstrates the value Fellows bring to partner organizations.

More Than 200 Fellows Placed

The program has already supported over 200 placements across multiple sectors.

Eight Fellowship Cohorts

Over eight cohorts, the fellowship has built a strong reputation among employers and mission-driven organizations.

Where Fellows Go After the Program

Graduates of the fellowship pursue careers across a wide range of sectors.

These include:

Government and Public Sector

Working on:

  • Public policy
  • Data governance
  • Service delivery improvement
  • Public administration

Strategy Consulting

Helping organizations solve complex challenges through data-driven strategies.

Nonprofits and Foundations

Supporting social impact initiatives through analytics and evaluation.

Research and Academia

Conducting advanced research and contributing to evidence-based policy.

Technology and Data Industry

Building innovative products, systems, and AI solutions.

Understanding the Selection Process

The Applied Data Fellowship is highly selective.

The program reports reviewing over 1,400 applicants while selecting only a small percentage of candidates.

Step 1: Rolling Application

Applications are accepted throughout the year.

Candidates submit:

  • Professional background information
  • Technical qualifications
  • Relevant experience
  • Mission-driven interests

There is no fixed application deadline.

Step 2: Strategic Profile Review

Unlike traditional programs, applicants are not immediately interviewed after applying.

Instead, profiles are reviewed against active partner organization needs.

Candidates are invited to proceed only when there is strong alignment between their expertise and multiple available projects.

Step 3: Interview Sprint

Once selected for interviews, the process moves quickly.

Most interviews are completed within approximately two weeks.

Candidates may receive one of three outcomes:

Yes

An offer to join the fellowship.

No

The application is not advanced.

Maybe

The candidate demonstrates strong potential but does not currently align with available projects.

Their profile remains active for future matching opportunities.

Step 4: Matching Process

Following acceptance, Fellows enter the matching phase.

This process generally takes:

  • 6 to 8 weeks

During this period, Fellows are paired with host organizations that best fit their skills and interests.

Step 5: Fellowship Placement

Once matching is completed, Fellows begin their:

  • Full-time
  • One-year fellowship engagement

within their assigned organization.

Fellowship Timeline at a Glance

Application Window

Open year-round

Interview Process

Approximately 2 weeks after invitation

Offer to Placement

6–8 weeks

Fellowship Duration

1 year full-time

Why You Should Apply

The Applied Data Fellowship offers much more than professional experience.

It provides:

  • Real-world impact
  • Hands-on data work
  • Exposure to AI and emerging technologies
  • Personalized mentorship
  • Professional training
  • Career acceleration
  • Access to mission-driven organizations
  • Networking opportunities with industry leaders
  • Potential full-time employment after completion

Apply Now

For aspiring data professionals who want to combine technical excellence with meaningful public impact, few opportunities offer the depth, exposure, and career potential of the Applied Data Fellowship.

Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible to maximize matching opportunities and increase their chances of placement.

Learn more, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post The Applied Data Fellowship 2026–2027: Launch Your Career in Data, AI, Public Policy, and Social Impact Through the University of Chicago Harris School of Public Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and BER Economist Internship Programme 2027: Fully Paid Training Opportunity for Aspiring Economists
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
For ambitious economics graduates seeking to launch a career in economic research, monetary policy, forecasting, and data-driven economic analysis, the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and the Bureau for Economic Research (BER) have opened applications for the highly competitive Economist Internship Programme 2027. This prestigious trainee programme offers successful candidates the opportunity to gain practical […]

The post South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and BER Economist Internship Programme 2027: Fully Paid Training Opportunity for Aspiring Economists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
For ambitious economics graduates seeking to launch a career in economic research, monetary policy, forecasting, and data-driven economic analysis, the South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and the Bureau for Economic Research (BER) have opened applications for the highly competitive Economist Internship Programme 2027.

This prestigious trainee programme offers successful candidates the opportunity to gain practical experience while working within one of South Africa’s most respected economic institutions. Participants will receive full-time employment with the South African Reserve Bank while undergoing intensive training at the Bureau for Economic Research at Stellenbosch University throughout 2027.

The programme is designed to develop the next generation of economists who will contribute to economic policy, forecasting, research, and data analysis in South Africa.

Successful candidates will not only gain invaluable professional experience but will also receive a competitive salary during the training year.

For graduates passionate about economics, macroeconomic analysis, research, and policy development, this programme represents an exceptional career-building opportunity.

About the SARB/BER Economist Internship Programme

The Economist Internship Programme is a collaborative initiative between the South African Reserve Bank and the Bureau for Economic Research.

The programme is specifically designed to identify, train, and develop talented economics graduates with strong analytical capabilities and a deep interest in economic research and policy.

Successful applicants will become full-time employees of the South African Reserve Bank while spending their first year in structured training at the Bureau for Economic Research in Stellenbosch.

Training Period

The internship runs for a full year:

  • Start Date: January 2027
  • End Date: December 2027

During this period, trainees will be exposed to advanced economic analysis, forecasting methodologies, research techniques, and practical policy-related work.

Why This Internship Matters

Economic policy plays a critical role in shaping a country’s growth, employment, inflation, and financial stability.

Institutions such as the South African Reserve Bank require highly skilled economists who can:

  • Analyze economic trends
  • Conduct macroeconomic research
  • Build forecasting models
  • Interpret economic data
  • Support policy decision-making
  • Monitor inflation and growth indicators
  • Contribute to monetary policy discussions

The SARB/BER Internship Programme is specifically designed to prepare young economists for these responsibilities.

Participants gain hands-on exposure to the practical application of economics in a real-world policy environment.

What Makes This Opportunity Unique?

Several features distinguish this internship from traditional graduate programmes.

1. Full-Time Employment with SARB

Successful candidates are not merely interns.

They become full-time employees of the South African Reserve Bank while participating in structured training.

This provides:

  • Professional work experience
  • Employment security during the programme
  • Exposure to central banking operations
  • Access to professional networks

2. Competitive Salary

Participants receive a competitive salary throughout the training year.

This allows trainees to focus fully on professional development and learning without the financial constraints often associated with internships.

3. Intensive Economic Research Training

Training at the Bureau for Economic Research provides exposure to:

  • Economic forecasting
  • Policy analysis
  • Research methodologies
  • Data interpretation
  • Economic modelling
  • Macroeconomic analysis

4. Career Development Opportunities

Graduates gain valuable experience that can open doors to careers in:

  • Central banking
  • Government economic departments
  • Research institutions
  • Financial institutions
  • Economic consulting
  • Academia
  • International organizations

Key Areas of Learning

Participants can expect exposure to a broad range of economic disciplines and analytical tools.

These may include:

Macroeconomic Analysis

Understanding and analyzing:

  • Economic growth
  • Inflation
  • Interest rates
  • Employment trends
  • Fiscal policy
  • Monetary policy

Economic Forecasting

Learning to:

  • Develop forecasts
  • Analyze economic indicators
  • Build predictive models
  • Evaluate economic risks

Policy Research

Supporting evidence-based policy development through:

  • Literature reviews
  • Data analysis
  • Economic modelling
  • Policy recommendations

Data Analytics

Using modern analytical tools to:

  • Process large datasets
  • Interpret economic information
  • Generate insights for decision-makers

Data Science for Economics Track

The programme also offers opportunities for candidates interested in the growing field of Data Science for Economics.

This specialized pathway focuses on combining economic expertise with advanced quantitative and computational methods.

Applicants interested in this component must demonstrate additional capabilities, including:

  • Programming experience
  • Quantitative coursework
  • Statistical analysis skills
  • Data science competencies
  • GitHub portfolio or coding experience

The increasing importance of big data and machine learning in economics makes this track particularly attractive for future-focused economists.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet all stated requirements.

Citizenship Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be South African citizens

Academic Requirements

Candidates must possess:

Option 1: Honours Degree

  • Honours degree in Economics completed by January 2027

OR

Option 2: Master’s Degree

  • Master’s degree in Economics completed by January 2027

In both cases:

  • Macroeconomics must have been studied at postgraduate level

Language Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be fluent in English

Professional Competencies

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Excellent communication skills
  • Strong writing abilities
  • Analytical thinking
  • Research aptitude
  • Problem-solving capabilities

Additional Requirements for Data Science Applicants

Candidates interested in the Data Science stream should possess:

  • Quantitative coursework
  • Programming skills
  • Coding experience
  • GitHub portfolio (preferred)
  • Data analysis capabilities

Selection Process

The recruitment process is designed to identify highly capable and motivated candidates.

Interview Stage

Interviews are scheduled to take place during:

  • July 2026
  • August 2026

Additional Assessments

Shortlisted candidates will undergo:

  1. Psychometric assessments
  2. Reference checks
  3. Security clearance procedures

These assessments help ensure that selected candidates meet the professional standards expected by the South African Reserve Bank.

Diversity and Inclusion

Candidate selection will be guided by the South African Reserve Bank’s commitment to workforce diversification.

The programme seeks to create opportunities while building a workforce that reflects the diversity of South Africa.

Benefits of the Programme

Participants can expect numerous professional benefits, including:

Professional Benefits

  • Full-time employment with SARB
  • Competitive salary
  • Structured economist training
  • Research experience
  • Economic forecasting exposure
  • Policy analysis experience

Technical Benefits

  • Advanced economic modelling skills
  • Quantitative analysis training
  • Data interpretation expertise
  • Forecasting methodologies
  • Research techniques

Career Benefits

  • Enhanced employability
  • Exposure to central banking
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Access to leading economists
  • Long-term career development prospects

Deadline:

Applications are rolling and early applications are encouraged.

Why Economics Graduates Should Apply

Few graduate programmes in South Africa offer the combination of:

  • Full-time employment
  • Structured training
  • Competitive remuneration
  • Research exposure
  • Policy engagement
  • Central banking experience

Learn more and Apply here

For students and graduates seeking a meaningful and impactful economics career, the SARB/BER Economist Internship Programme provides an exceptional foundation.

Participants will gain the skills, knowledge, and professional networks needed to thrive in economic policy, research, forecasting, and financial sector careers.

The programme represents an outstanding opportunity to learn from leading economists while contributing to South Africa’s economic future.

Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post South African Reserve Bank (SARB) and BER Economist Internship Programme 2027: Fully Paid Training Opportunity for Aspiring Economists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026: 21 Prestigious Faculty Positions in Medicine and Health Open to Global Researchers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Karolinska Institutet (KI), one of the world’s leading medical universities and the institution responsible for selecting Nobel Prize laureates in Physiology or Medicine, has officially launched its highly anticipated Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026. The international recruitment initiative aims to attract outstanding early-career researchers from around the world to join KI’s thriving scientific community. Building […]

The post Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026: 21 Prestigious Faculty Positions in Medicine and Health Open to Global Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Karolinska Institutet (KI), one of the world’s leading medical universities and the institution responsible for selecting Nobel Prize laureates in Physiology or Medicine, has officially launched its highly anticipated Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026.

The international recruitment initiative aims to attract outstanding early-career researchers from around the world to join KI’s thriving scientific community. Building on the remarkable success of previous recruitment campaigns, Karolinska Institutet is offering 21 Assistant Professor positions across a broad spectrum of medicine, health sciences, biomedical research, clinical sciences, and public health disciplines.

The programme provides successful candidates with generous start-up funding, access to world-class research infrastructure, a clear pathway toward permanent academic positions, and the opportunity to contribute to groundbreaking research at one of Europe’s most prestigious medical universities.

Why the Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Programme Matters

Karolinska Institutet continues its long-term strategy of strengthening its position as a global leader in medical research and innovation. Through this recruitment programme, KI seeks exceptional researchers capable of driving scientific discoveries that improve human health and transform medical practice.

The initiative is designed specifically for talented early-career scientists who are ready to establish independent research programmes while benefiting from an internationally renowned academic environment.

Unlike many temporary academic appointments, this programme offers a structured career development pathway that can lead to promotion as a Senior Lecturer, creating a stable and rewarding long-term academic career.

What Successful Candidates Will Receive

Researchers selected through the Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme will benefit from an exceptional package of academic and professional support.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Generous start-up research funding
  • Opportunity to establish an independent research group
  • Access to cutting-edge research facilities
  • Internationally recognized academic environment
  • Collaboration opportunities with leading researchers
  • Strong links between research and clinical practice
  • Access to advanced biomedical technologies
  • Comprehensive research support services
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaborations
  • Clear pathway toward a permanent Senior Lecturer position
  • Academic freedom and research independence
  • Work-life balance benefits associated with Sweden’s academic system
  • Support for innovation and commercialization of research discoveries
  • Access to the KI Research Incubator (KIRI)
  • Opportunities to collaborate with healthcare systems and clinical partners

Available Assistant Professor Positions in Basic Sciences

Karolinska Institutet is recruiting researchers across numerous biomedical and laboratory-based disciplines.

Open Positions

  1. Assistant Professor in Biomedical Science
  2. Assistant Professor in Cell and Molecular Biology
  3. Assistant Professor in Experimental Translational Medicine
  4. Assistant Professor in Infection and Immunity or Metabolism, Liver Disease, and Nutrition
  5. Assistant Professor in Immuno-Oncology
  6. Assistant Professor in Inflammatory Diseases
  7. Assistant Professor in Microbiology Focusing on Bacteriology
  8. Assistant Professor in Precision Medicine
  9. Assistant Professor in Neuroscience
  10. Assistant Professor in Pharmacology
  11. Assistant Professor in Reproductive Systems Biology
  12. Assistant Professor in Tumour Biology

These positions provide opportunities for scientists working at the forefront of biomedical innovation to establish internationally competitive research programmes.

Available Assistant Professor Positions in Clinical Sciences and Public Health

Researchers with expertise in clinical medicine, epidemiology, public health, and healthcare-related disciplines are also encouraged to apply.

Open Positions

  1. Assistant Professor in Clinical Oral Sciences
  2. Assistant Professor in Clinical Therapy Research
  3. Assistant Professor in Data-Driven Cardiovascular Medicine
  4. Assistant Professor at the Department of Clinical Science, Intervention and Technology
  5. Assistant Professor in Environmental Medicine
  6. Assistant Professor in Epidemiology or Biostatistics
  7. Assistant Professor in Mental and Sexual Health
  8. Assistant Professor in Older Adults’ Health
  9. Assistant Professor in Psychiatric Pharmacoepidemiology

These positions emphasize translational research and the integration of scientific discoveries into healthcare practice.

Candidates May Apply for Multiple Positions

One of the major advantages of the recruitment programme is that applicants are welcome to submit applications for more than one Assistant Professor position.

This flexibility enables researchers whose expertise spans multiple disciplines to maximize their chances of securing a faculty appointment and allows KI to identify candidates whose research aligns with several strategic priorities.

Recruitment Timeline and Important Dates

Applicants should carefully note the official recruitment schedule.

Key Dates

Faculty Board Decision

  • 1 April 2026

Application Period

  • 1 June 2026 – 31 August 2026

Application Deadline

  • 31 August 2026

Assessment Process

  • September 2026 – January 2027

Planned Starting Date

  • 2027

Candidates are encouraged to prepare their application materials early to ensure a competitive submission before the deadline.

A Truly Global Recruitment Initiative

Karolinska Institutet’s Assistant Professor initiative has demonstrated significant international appeal.

The previous recruitment campaign attracted more than 2,700 applications from researchers representing nearly 90 countries worldwide, highlighting the university’s reputation as a premier destination for scientific talent.

The 2026 recruitment round is expected to attract another highly competitive and diverse applicant pool from across Europe, Africa, Asia, North America, South America, and Oceania.

World-Class Research Environment at Karolinska Institutet

Karolinska Institutet accounts for the largest share of academic medical research conducted in Sweden.

Its research activities span the entire biomedical spectrum, including:

  • Basic biomedical research
  • Molecular biology
  • Clinical medicine
  • Translational research
  • Population health
  • Public health
  • Precision medicine
  • Epidemiology
  • Neuroscience
  • Immunology
  • Pharmacology
  • Healthcare innovation

Researchers benefit from an ecosystem specifically designed to accelerate scientific discovery and translation into healthcare solutions.

Access to Advanced Research Infrastructure

A major attraction of working at KI is access to extensive core facilities that provide advanced technologies, services, and expertise.

Researchers can utilize:

  • High-end imaging facilities
  • Live-cell imaging platforms
  • Genomics and sequencing technologies
  • Bioinformatics support
  • Clinical research resources
  • Advanced laboratory technologies
  • Data science infrastructure
  • Specialized biomedical equipment

These facilities continue to evolve to ensure researchers have access to the latest scientific methods and technologies.

KI Research Incubator (KIRI)

Innovation is a central component of Karolinska Institutet’s mission.

The KI Research Incubator (KIRI) serves as a platform dedicated to supporting emerging researchers by:

  • Encouraging scientific innovation
  • Promoting interdisciplinary collaborations
  • Supporting entrepreneurship
  • Facilitating research commercialization
  • Connecting researchers with industry partners

This environment allows researchers to transform scientific discoveries into practical health solutions with real-world impact.

Career Development and Academic Advancement

Karolinska Institutet is committed to supporting researchers throughout their academic careers.

Faculty members gain access to:

  • Research grants and awards
  • Recruitment support for doctoral students
  • Junior Faculty networks
  • Promotion pathways
  • Mentorship opportunities
  • Pedagogical development programmes
  • Research leadership training
  • Innovation support services

These opportunities create a strong foundation for long-term academic success.

Why Researchers Choose Karolinska Institutet

Many scientists are attracted to KI because of its unique combination of:

  • Academic freedom
  • International prestige
  • Research excellence
  • Strong clinical partnerships
  • Innovation ecosystem
  • Career progression opportunities
  • Collaborative environment
  • Access to global research networks

The institution consistently ranks among Europe’s leading medical universities and continues to shape the future of medicine through world-class education, research, and innovation.

Success Stories from Newly Recruited Assistant Professors

Recent Assistant Professors at KI demonstrate the global nature and scientific excellence of the programme.

Examples include researchers specializing in:

  • Pregnancy drug safety research
  • Stem cell biology
  • Organoid technologies
  • Genome engineering
  • Neuroimaging
  • Multi-omics research
  • Human immunology
  • Genetic precision medicine

These scholars joined KI from leading institutions across Europe and North America, bringing diverse expertise and perspectives to the university’s research community.

Final Thoughts

The Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026 represents one of the most attractive international faculty recruitment opportunities currently available in the global health and biomedical research landscape.

With 21 positions available, generous research funding, world-class infrastructure, strong clinical integration, and a pathway toward permanent academic employment, the programme offers an exceptional opportunity for ambitious early-career researchers seeking to establish themselves among the world’s leading scientific communities.

Researchers with innovative ideas, strong publication records, and a passion for advancing medicine and human health are strongly encouraged to apply before the 31 August 2026 deadline.

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Karolinska Institutet Assistant Professor Recruitment Programme 2026: 21 Prestigious Faculty Positions in Medicine and Health Open to Global Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
